facts: We see with our eyes because they take in light, but we hear with our ears because they take in sound — each sense has its own organ.
Our skin lets us feel touch, so we can tell if something is rough or smooth, but only our tongue can taste food.
We smell with our nose; for example, we know a flower is sweet because our nose detects the smell, not our tongue.
misconceptions: Students believe that they taste food with their nose because they smell it while eating, but only the tongue can detect taste.
